{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant Manager, Pre-Delivery Inspection",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Manager, Pre-Delivery Inspection

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Assistant Manager, Pre-Delivery Inspection to oversee vehicle inspection processes and ensure all automobiles meet quality and safety standards before delivery to customers.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and manage pre-delivery inspection (PDI) activities
	Ensure all vehicles meet quality, safety, and compliance standards
	Coordinate with technical, sales, and service teams to resolve issues before delivery
	Maintain accurate inspection records and reports
	Identify and escalate defects, ensuring timely resolution
	Improve inspection processes to enhance efficiency and quality


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or relevant qualification
	Proven experience in the automobile industry (mandatory)
	Strong knowledge of vehicle systems and inspection processes
	Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills
	Good leadership and team management ability.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to&nbsp;cv@ascentech.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Assistant Manager, Pre-Delivery Inspection Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Manager, Pre-Delivery Inspection

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Assistant Manager, Pre-Delivery Inspection to oversee vehicle inspection processes and ensure all automobiles meet quality and safety standards before delivery to customers.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and manage pre-delivery inspection (PDI) activities
	Ensure all vehicles meet quality, safety, and compliance standards
	Coordinate with technical, sales, and service teams to resolve issues before delivery
	Maintain accurate inspection records and reports
	Identify and escalate defects, ensuring timely resolution
	Improve inspection processes to enhance efficiency and quality


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or relevant qualification
	Proven experience in the automobile industry (mandatory)
	Strong knowledge of vehicle systems and inspection processes
	Excellent attention to detail and problem-solving skills
	Good leadership and team management ability.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) Associate",
  "description": "Savannah Energy is a young, high growth, highly ambitious energy company. We are focused on operating in countries where we can make a difference to local communities, where we can play a strategic role in the energy sector and where we can make a positive impact on the socio-economic development of the country.

Our vision is to create a best in class African-focused energy company that we and our stakeholders are proud of. Our five core values of Sustainability, Excellence, Entrepreneurialism, Integrity and Teamwork define the essence of Savannah and underpin our corporate culture.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&amp;A) Associate

Job Location: Lagos

&nbsp;Job Purpose / Objectives


	To support the Financial Planning &amp; Analysis (FP&amp;A) team in driving financial planning, performance, forecasting, going concern assessments, annual budgeting, and strategic analysis across the organization.
	To assist in the preparation of key financial reports, support business decision-making processes, and help ensure the accuracy and timeliness of financial data.


Duties &amp; Responsibilities


	Budgeting and Forecasting: Assist in the preparation, coordination and review of the annual budget and periodic forecasts. Ensure alignment with business objectives and provide insights on financial performance.
	Financial Reporting: Support the preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports for internal stakeholders. Assist in presenting financial results and providing recommendations for improvement.
	Variance Analysis: Analyse variances between actual and forecasted financials, helping to identify trends and drivers of performance. Work with business units to explain discrepancies and recommend corrective actions.
	Data Management and Reporting Tools: Maintain and update financial models, databases, and reports to track business performance. Ensure accurate and timely reporting of key metrics, KPIs, and other relevant financial data.
	Process Improvement: Assist in streamlining financial reporting and forecasting processes. Identify areas for efficiency improvement and work with the FP&amp;A team to implement best practices.
	Collaboration and Communication: Work closely with cross-functional teams (Commercial, Operations, HR, etc.) to gather necessary data, provide financial insights, and assist in decision-making. Foster collaborative relationships across departments.
	Ad-hoc Analysis: Support various ad-hoc financial analysis projects, as requested by senior leadership, to assist in strategic planning, investment decisions, and cost management.
	Month-End Close Support: Help facilitate month-end and year-end close processes by gathering data and providing necessary documentation to support accurate financial reporting.
	Going concern assessment: Annually as part of the entities&rsquo; going concern assumption for audit assessment, prepare the going concern model together with headroom sensitivities analysis.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Line Manager.


Job &amp; Main Specifications (Education/Knowledge, Skills, Attributes, Experience &amp; Other Required Details)


	University degree in Business Administration, Finance, Accounting, Economics or related discipline with a minimum of five years&rsquo; experience.
	Professional qualification such as ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent is required.
	Excellent organisational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Intermediate to advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office 365 i.e., Microsoft Excel, Outlook, Word, PowerPoint etc. with advanced proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, X/V-LOOKUP, INDEX MATCH, financial modelling).
	Experience of ERP systems such as SAP and Oracle is advantageous for data extraction purposes.
	Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and interact with stakeholders at various levels.
	Be willing to live our values and have a passion for &quot;Projects that Matter&quot;.&nbsp;


Note:&nbsp;Only qualified candidates will be contacted.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-30",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-31",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) Associate Job at Savannah Energy




        
    

        
        Savannah Energy is a young, high growth, highly ambitious energy company. We are focused on operating in countries where we can make a difference to local communities, where we can play a strategic role in the energy sector and where we can make a positive impact on the socio-economic development of the country.

Our vision is to create a best in class African-focused energy company that we and our stakeholders are proud of. Our five core values of Sustainability, Excellence, Entrepreneurialism, Integrity and Teamwork define the essence of Savannah and underpin our corporate culture.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) Associate
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos

 Job Purpose / Objectives


	To support the Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) team in driving financial planning, performance, forecasting, going concern assessments, annual budgeting, and strategic analysis across the organization.
	To assist in the preparation of key financial reports, support business decision-making processes, and help ensure the accuracy and timeliness of financial data.


Duties & Responsibilities


	Budgeting and Forecasting: Assist in the preparation, coordination and review of the annual budget and periodic forecasts. Ensure alignment with business objectives and provide insights on financial performance.
	Financial Reporting: Support the preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports for internal stakeholders. Assist in presenting financial results and providing recommendations for improvement.
	Variance Analysis: Analyse variances between actual and forecasted financials, helping to identify trends and drivers of performance. Work with business units to explain discrepancies and recommend corrective actions.
	Data Management and Reporting Tools: Maintain and update financial models, databases, and reports to track business performance. Ensure accurate and timely reporting of key metrics, KPIs, and other relevant financial data.
	Process Improvement: Assist in streamlining financial reporting and forecasting processes. Identify areas for efficiency improvement and work with the FP&A team to implement best practices.
	Collaboration and Communication: Work closely with cross-functional teams (Commercial, Operations, HR, etc.) to gather necessary data, provide financial insights, and assist in decision-making. Foster collaborative relationships across departments.
	Ad-hoc Analysis: Support various ad-hoc financial analysis projects, as requested by senior leadership, to assist in strategic planning, investment decisions, and cost management.
	Month-End Close Support: Help facilitate month-end and year-end close processes by gathering data and providing necessary documentation to support accurate financial reporting.
	Going concern assessment: Annually as part of the entities’ going concern assumption for audit assessment, prepare the going concern model together with headroom sensitivities analysis.    
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Line Manager.


Job & Main Specifications (Education/Knowledge, Skills, Attributes, Experience & Other Required Details)


	University degree in Business Administration, Finance, Accounting, Economics or related discipline with a minimum of five years’ experience.
	Professional qualification such as ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent is required.
	Excellent organisational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Intermediate to advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office 365 i.e., Microsoft Excel, Outlook, Word, PowerPoint etc. with advanced proficiency in Excel (pivot tables, X/V-LOOKUP, INDEX MATCH, financial modelling).
	Experience of ERP systems such as SAP and Oracle is advantageous for data extraction purposes.
	Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and interact with stakeholders at various levels.
	Be willing to live our values and have a passion for "Projects that Matter". 


Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: CNG Specialist

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time

	We are seeking a skilled CNG Specialist for a full-time, on-site role based in Lagos.


Responsibilities

title: Job Vacancies at Digital Ehealth Solutions Limited
content:
Digital Ehealth Solutions Limited is an ICT service company with experience in enhancing health management systems and healthcare-focused services through digital information, to improve healthcare outcomes amongst other value added services.

Our approach emphasizes client satisfaction and high-quality service delivery, supported by a team of forward-thinking professionals who design, develop, and deploy tailored ICT infrastructure for efficient healthcare delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Technical Compliance Team

Job Location: Abuja (FCT), Delta
Employment Type:

Roles & Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Ensure proper installation, configuration, training, and quality assurance of the digital eHealth software.
	Ensure all devices (mobile, desktops, printers, etc.) are in good working condition and handle repairs when necessary.
	Resolve technical issues related to software or devices as they arise from the field.
	Document, track, and resolve issues or service requests from clients.
	Escalate complex issues to the software development team when required.


Qualifications
Education:


	Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Cybersecurity, or a related field.
	Advanced degrees (e.g., Master’s) or certifications in compliance, risk management, or information security are an added advantage.


Certifications:


	Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA).
	Certified Information Security Manager (CISM).
	Other relevant certifications such as Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH), or CompTIA Security+ are beneficial.


Knowledge & Skills:


	Strong knowledge of privacy-by-design principles and integration of privacy controls into technical systems and software development processes.
	Experience conducting technical risk assessments, vulnerability assessments, and penetration testing.
	Proficiency in implementing and managing technical controls such as access control, encryption, logging and monitoring, data masking, and data loss prevention (DLP).
	Familiarity with cloud computing platforms and security best practices (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud Platform).
	Experience with identity and access management (IAM), including role-based access control (RBAC) and multi-factor authentication (MFA).
	Strong understanding of network security principles, including firewalls, intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS), and secure network architecture.
	Knowledge of secure software development practices and secure coding principles.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV, cover letter, and copies of relevant certifications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.


2.) Data Protection Team Officer

Job Locations: Abuja (FCT) and Delta
Employment Type: Full Time

Roles


	Ensure all data received on the server are complaints with NPC and NDDC required data.
	Ensure all household and enrolee records are properly taken.
	All required are properly filled.
	Give weekly reports on data ac?vi?es e.g. data synced (compliant and non-compliant), users, agents and clinical data etc. Regular update of the company’s data protec?on policies in alignment with NDPR data and compliance and regula?ons policies. Perform necessary training for all staffs in terms of data compliance and privacy policies.
	Responsible for onboarding of agents.


Required Qualifications


	 Education: Bachelor's Degree in a relevant field such as Computer Science, Information Technology, Law, Business Administration, or a related discipline. 
	Advanced degrees (e.g., Master's, MBA) or certifications in areas such as data protection, privacy law, compliance, or risk management may be advantageous.
	Certifications: 
	Certified Information Privacy Professional (CIPP) certification from the International Association of Privacy Professionals (IAPP). Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP) certification from (ISC).
	Other relevant certifications such as Certified Information Security Manager (CISM), Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA), or Certified Compliance and Ethics Professional (CCEP) may also be beneficial. 
	Knowledge and Skills: Understanding of Nigeria's data protection laws and regulations, including the Nigeria Data Protection Regulation (NDPR) and other relevant legislation. 
	Familiarity with regional and international data protection frameworks such as the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), and their implications for Nigerian organizations. 
	Knowledge of privacy-by-design principles and best practices for implementing privacy controls in systems and processes.
	Ability to conduct privacy impact assessments (PIAs), data protection impact assessments (DPIAs), and audits to evaluate compliance with data protection laws and regulations.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated Resume, cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.



3.) Technical Implementation Team Officer

Job Locations: Abuja (FCT) and Delta
Employment Type: Full Time

Roles


	Ensure tasks on all projects are completed properly within the timeline.
	Ensure proper resolution? ons of issues with databases, e.g., database design, management, and optimization.
	Ensure, monitor, and enforce compliance with policies and regulations on company policies from various departments.
	Ensure Devices are well configured and agents are properly onboarded
	Fix bugs and software optimization.


Required Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field.
	Advanced Degrees (e.g., Master's) or certifications in areas such as project management, software development, or specific technical domains may be advantageous.
	Certifications: Project Management Professional (PMP) certification or other project management certifications.
	Certifications in relevant technologies or platforms such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect, AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA), etc.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, PHP, Java, JavaScript, or C. Experience with software development frameworks and tools such as Angular, React, Node.js, .NET, Laravel, or Django.
	Knowledge of database systems and SQL, including database design, optimization, and management.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated resume and cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Resurgir Consulting - Our client is seeking reliable and detail-oriented individuals to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;Egbeda, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Onsite)
Working Hours: Monday &ndash; Saturday, 8:00 AM &ndash; 5:00 PM
Start Date: Immediate

Job Summary


	They are seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Accountant to manage the company&rsquo;s financial records and ensure accurate financial reporting.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining proper accounting documentation, processing financial transactions, and supporting the company&rsquo;s financial operations with a high level of confidentiality and professionalism.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure all transactions are properly documented.
	Prepare and manage financial reports, including daily, weekly, and monthly records.
	Record and reconcile financial transactions using accounting software such as Busy Accounting Software.
	Monitor company income and expenditures to ensure proper financial management.
	Reconcile bank statements and resolve any discrepancies.
	Maintain strict confidentiality of financial information and company records.
	Assist management in budgeting and financial planning.
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards and company financial policies.
	Support audit processes and provide necessary financial documentation when required.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc Degree in Accounting or a related field.
	2&ndash;3 years of relevant work experience as an Accountant.
	Proficiency in the use of accounting software such as Busy Accounting Software.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Ability to handle sensitive financial information with strict confidentiality.
	Must be loyal, honest, punctual, and proactive.
	Good organizational and time-management skills.


Key Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	High level of integrity and accountability
	Excellent attention to detail
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Salary
N120,000 Monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;applyresurgir@gmail.com&nbsp;using &ldquo;Accountant&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.",

  },

Accountant Job at Resurgir Consulting




        
    

        
        Resurgir Consulting - Our client is seeking reliable and detail-oriented individuals to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location: Egbeda, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Onsite)
Working Hours: Monday – Saturday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
Start Date: Immediate

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	They are seeking a reliable and detail-oriented Accountant to manage the company’s financial records and ensure accurate financial reporting.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining proper accounting documentation, processing financial transactions, and supporting the company’s financial operations with a high level of confidentiality and professionalism.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure all transactions are properly documented.
	Prepare and manage financial reports, including daily, weekly, and monthly records.
	Record and reconcile financial transactions using accounting software such as Busy Accounting Software.
	Monitor company income and expenditures to ensure proper financial management.
	Reconcile bank statements and resolve any discrepancies.
	Maintain strict confidentiality of financial information and company records.
	Assist management in budgeting and financial planning.
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards and company financial policies.
	Support audit processes and provide necessary financial documentation when required.


Requirements


	Minimum of HND or B.Sc Degree in Accounting or a related field.
	2–3 years of relevant work experience as an Accountant.
	Proficiency in the use of accounting software such as Busy Accounting Software.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Ability to handle sensitive financial information with strict confidentiality.
	Must be loyal, honest, punctual, and proactive.
	Good organizational and time-management skills.


Key Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	High level of integrity and accountability
	Excellent attention to detail
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Salary
N120,000 Monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Accountant” as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
